content:
First we index the set  
    M = {1,2,4,5,7,8,…}  
by  
    m₁=1, m₂=2,  
    m₃=4, m₄=5,  
    m₅=7, m₆=8, …  
Equivalently, in “blocks” of two: for t≥1,  
    m_{2t−1}=3t−2,   m_{2t}=3t−1.  

We wish to find all n≥1 for which there is a run of 2n consecutive m_k summing to 300.  One checks two cases:

Case 1 start at an odd index, k₀=2t−1.  Then the 2n terms are exactly the two from block t and then the next n−1 full blocks, giving sum  
    S=3n·(2t+n−2)=300  
⇔ n(2t+n−2)=100.  
Since n|100 and 2t must be integer ≥2, one finds the only positive n dividing 100 with 100/n−n+2 even are n=2 or 10.

Case 2 start at an even index, k₀=2t.  Then one takes the second element of block t, then n−1 full blocks, then the first element of block t+n, giving  
    S=3n·(2t+n−1)=300  
⇔ n(2t+n−1)=100.  
Again n|100 and 100/n−n+1 must be even; this forces n=1,4,5.

In fact one checks all five of these work.  Hence the possible values of n are

    n=1, 2, 4, 5, 10.

Answer: \boxed{1,2,4,5,10}.
